title: Basic Anatomy Question
stem: Which of the following structures is responsible for pumping blood throughout the body?
teaching_point: The heart is a muscular organ that acts as a pump to circulate blood throughout the cardiovascular system. It has four chambers and uses rhythmic contractions to maintain blood flow.

answer_options.explanation: The liver is responsible for metabolism and detoxification, not blood circulation.
Correct! The heart is the central pump of the cardiovascular system, responsible for circulating blood throughout the body.
The lungs are responsible for gas exchange (oxygen and carbon dioxide), not pumping blood.
The kidneys filter blood and produce urine, but they do not pump blood.
